paper_title: Elasmobranch Fossil Collection from the Achen Formation

methods.description: Process for recovering elasmobranch specimens (including teeth and tail spines) from the Auwiesholz Member of the Achen Formation in the Simssee area, Bavaria, S. Germany, using screen washing and surface collecting techniques. The method includes accessing the site, performing the collection, recording contextual information, and housing the specimens in a designated repository.

steps.description: Access the collection site along the Auwiesholz Member of the Achen Formation in the Simssee area (Bavaria, S. Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and access geological collection site for fossil recovery.
inputs: Geological location information (Auwiesholz Member, Achen Formation, Simssee area, Bavaria, S. Germany)

steps.description: Recover elasmobranch specimens (including teeth and tail spines) by screen washing and surface collecting from the site.
steps.searchable_summary: Recover fossil specimens using screen washing and surface collecting techniques.
outputs: Raw collected elasmobranch fossil specimens (teeth, tail spines)

steps.description: Record contextual information including the precise stratigraphic origin (though ambiguous for all material), age (middle Ottnangian, ca. 17.8 Ma), and depositional setting (inner neritic environment).
steps.searchable_summary: Record geological and stratigraphic context for collected fossil specimens.
outputs: Specimen records with associated contextual data (stratigraphy, age, environment)

steps.description: House the collected fossils in the Bayerische Staatssammlung für Paläontologie und Geologie at Munich, Germany, assigning catalogue numbers with the prefix SNSB-BSPG 2019 III.
steps.searchable_summary: Store collected fossil specimens in a designated repository and assign catalogue numbers.
inputs: Repository information (Bayerische Staatssammlung für Paläontologie und Geologie)
outputs: Housed and catalogued fossil specimens in repository
